task: I need to get from Los Lunas to a couple of places in Albuquerque this weekend, and I don’t want to waste time taking the wrong route. Can you help me figure out the simplest way to plan the trip and what roads or landmarks I should keep an eye on?

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — the simplest way is to plan it as a **single route with 2–3 checkpoints**, then decide the order of your stops so you’re not backtracking.

## Quick way to plan it
1. **List the exact places in Albuquerque** you need to go.
2. **Put them in route order** — usually the stop closest to I-25 first, then farther east/west stops.
3. **Use one main highway as your spine**:
   - Most trips from **Los Lunas to Albuquerque** are easiest via **I-25 North**.
4. **Add only the key exit names or landmarks** you’ll need, so you don’t have to keep checking directions.

## Main roads to watch
- **I-25 North**: your main route into Albuquerque.
- **NM-6 / Main Street area in Los Lunas**: common local access to get to I-25.
- **Central Ave / I-40**: useful if your stops are in central/east/west Albuquerque.
- **Rio Bravo / Broadway / Cesar Chavez**: helpful depending on where in Albuquerque you’re headed.

## Landmarks that help you stay oriented
- **Isleta area** south of Albuquerque
- **Downtown Albuquerque / Old Town**
- **University area / UNM**
- **ABQ Uptown / Coronado area**
- **Sandia foothills** if you’re going east side

## Best simple planning method
Make a tiny note like this:
- **Stop 1:** [place] — take **I-25 N**, exit at [exit/landmark]
- **Stop 2:** [place] — from there, use [main road]
- **Return:** get back on **I-25 S** toward Los Lunas

## To avoid wasting time
- Don’t rely on street names alone if you’re unfamiliar with Albuquerque — use **highway exits + landmarks**.
- Check **traffic before you leave**, especially if you’ll be near downtown or I-25/I-40.
- If one stop is on the east side and another is downtown, do **downtown first or last** depending on traffic, to avoid crossing the city twice.

If you want, send me the **names or rough areas of the places** you’re going, and I can help you lay out the **simplest stop order and route**.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, but I need the actual places. Albuquerque is one thing, but the route changes a lot depending on if it’s downtown, west side, east side, whatever.

Right now I’d just do this:
- leave Los Lunas on **I-25 N**
- use **one stop near the highway first**
- then the farther one
- coming back, just stay on **I-25 S**

If one of the places is closer to **Central / I-40**, that’s a different mess than if it’s near **Rio Bravo** or **Uptown**. Give me the names or at least the area, and I can sort the order בלי wasting time.

turns.agent_response:
Exactly — the route depends a lot on **which side of Albuquerque** the stops are on.

If you send me the **actual place names** or even just the **neighborhood/area** for each one, I can sort them into the simplest order and tell you:

- which one to hit first
- whether to stay on **I-25**, cut over on **I-40/Central**, or use a local road
- the main **exits/landmarks** to watch for
- the easiest way back to **Los Lunas**

A quick format works best:

- **Stop 1:** name + area
- **Stop 2:** name + area
- **Optional Stop 3:** name + area

If you don’t know the exact address, just give me something like:
- downtown
- west side
- east side
- Uptown
- near Rio Bravo
- near UNM
- near Old Town

Then I’ll lay it out in a clean route order.

user_simulator.text:
Fine, here:

- Stop 1: Walmart on Coors Blvd NW, west side
- Stop 2: a doctor’s office near Presbyterian downtown, around 1st and Coal

That’s basically what I need ordered. If it matters, I’d rather not zigzag across town twice.

turns.agent_response:
Yes — with those two stops, the simplest order is:

1. **Walmart on Coors Blvd NW** first  
2. **Doctor’s office near Presbyterian downtown / 1st & Coal** second  
3. Then head back to **Los Lunas via I-25 S**

### Why this order
- **Coors Blvd NW** is on the **west side**, so it’s better to get that done before cutting across town.
- **Downtown / 1st & Coal** is farther east and closer to the core, so doing it second avoids extra zigzagging.

### Simple route plan
- Leave **Los Lunas on I-25 N**
- Stay on **I-25 N** into Albuquerque
- For **Walmart on Coors Blvd NW**, you’ll likely need to:
  - get to **I-40 W** or a west-side connector depending on the exact Walmart location
  - watch for **Coors Blvd** exits and signs for the **west side**
- After that, head **back east toward downtown**
  - use **I-40 E** or local arterials depending on traffic
  - watch for **Downtown / Central / 1st St / Coal Ave**
- After the doctor’s office, return on **I-25 S**

### Landmarks / roads to keep an eye on
- **I-25 / I-40 interchange**: important if you’re crossing between west side and downtown
- **Coors Blvd NW**: west-side access road
- **Downtown / Presbyterian / 1st & Coal**: your final stop area
- **Central Ave**: helpful reference if you need to orient yourself downtown

### Best mental map
- **South to north:** Los Lunas → Albuquerque
- **West side first**
- **Downtown second**
- **Back south on I-25**

If you want, I can turn this into a **very short turn-by-turn style checklist** you can save on your phone.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, that works. West side first, downtown second. No zigzagging.

Best simple order:
- **Stop 1:** Walmart on **Coors Blvd NW**
- **Stop 2:** doctor’s office near **Presbyterian downtown / 1st & Coal**
- **Back:** **I-25 S** to Los Lunas

What to watch:
- **I-25 N** out of Los Lunas
- **I-40 / Coors area** for the west-side stop
- **Downtown / Central / 1st St / Coal** for the doctor
- **I-25 / I-40 interchange** if you’re switching sides of town

That’s the cleanest route. Do not start by going downtown first, that just wastes time.
